data.title: Raoul Pal Highlights Key Metrics for Blockchain Valuation
data.description: Raoul Pal, a prominent figure in the financial sector, has identified two crucial metrics that will shape the future valuation of blockchain technology: the value transacted per user and the total number of users. According to NS3.AI, Pal emphasized that Metcalfe's Law serves as the most effective model for assessing blockchain valuation. He noted that the increasing use of stablecoins and tokenization is enhancing transaction values, while the growth of AI agents is contributing to an expanding user base.

data.title: AI TRENDS | EU Proposes Measures to Curb Google's Influence by Opening Search Data
data.description: On April 16, Jin10 reported that the European Union has proposed measures requiring Google to open its search data to competitors, including AI chatbot developers, in an effort to curb the influence of the U.S. tech giant. According to Jin10, the European Commission announced on Thursday that it has sent preliminary investigation results to Google's parent company, Alphabet, demanding compliance with the Digital Markets Act by the end of July. The proposed measures would allow third-party search engines access to data such as rankings and queries to "optimize services and compete with Google Search." If approved, this data could be made available to companies like OpenAI and Anthropic. Google's Senior Competition Legal Counsel, Claire Kelly, responded by stating that the company will "defend itself against overreach," arguing that the move "endangers privacy and security." The EU had already initiated a related review in January of this year. Additionally, Google faces potential penalties under the Digital Markets Act for favoring its own services and restricting app developers.

data.title: ECB Official Stresses Importance of Commitment to 2% Inflation Target
data.description: An official from the European Central Bank (ECB) emphasized the critical importance of maintaining a commitment to the 2% inflation target. According to Jin10, the official highlighted that clear communication regarding this target is essential for ensuring economic stability and guiding market expectations. The ECB's dedication to this inflation goal is seen as a cornerstone of its monetary policy strategy, aiming to foster sustainable economic growth and price stability across the Eurozone. The official's remarks underscore the ongoing efforts by the ECB to manage inflationary pressures and support the region's economic recovery.

data.title: U.S. Continues Efforts to Broker Ceasefire, Says Lebanese Presidential Office
data.description: The Lebanese presidential office has announced that the United States is actively working to facilitate a ceasefire. According to Odaily, this development highlights ongoing diplomatic efforts in the region. Further details on the progress of these efforts have not been disclosed.

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | Canadian Agency Forecasts Gold Prices to Peak in 2026
data.description: On April 16, Jin10 reported that Export Development Canada, a Canadian export credit agency, has released its spring outlook predicting that strong safe-haven demand will keep gold prices elevated this year. According to the agency, after reaching an average price of $3,435 per ounce in 2025, gold prices are expected to average around $4,990 per ounce in 2026. The agency anticipates that gold will peak in 2026, and as uncertainties ease and high prices suppress demand, prices are projected to gradually decline to an average of $4,689 per ounce in 2027. Over time, jewelry demand is expected to regain its traditional role as the main source of gold demand, while investment-driven volatility is likely to diminish.

data.title: ECB Officials: War Fundamentally Alters Inflation Outlook
data.description: The European Central Bank (ECB) has released its meeting minutes, revealing that officials believe the ongoing war has fundamentally changed the inflation outlook. According to Jin10, the ECB emphasized that the conflict has introduced significant uncertainties and challenges to the economic landscape, impacting inflation expectations. The officials noted that the war's effects on supply chains and energy prices are contributing to the altered inflation dynamics. The ECB is closely monitoring these developments to assess their implications for monetary policy and economic stability.

data.title: UK Financial Conduct Authority Seeks Input on Crypto Asset Regulation
data.description:
The UK Financial Conduct Authority (FCA) has released a new consultation paper on the regulation of crypto assets. According to BlockBeats, the consultation seeks opinions on the scope of regulated digital asset activities within the regulatory roadmap framework. Key areas of focus include stablecoin issuance, trading platforms, custody, and staking activities. The consultation is open to individuals, businesses, industry groups, policymakers, and academics, with a deadline of June 3, 2026. Crypto firms can begin applying for FCA authorization from September 30, 2026.

This consultation marks the latest development in the UK's crypto regulatory roadmap. Earlier this year, the Financial Services and Markets Act 2000 (Crypto Assets) Regulations 2026 came into effect, bringing new types of crypto asset activities under FCA oversight. The FCA has indicated that substantial work on the future crypto regulatory framework's rules is nearly complete, with related policy statements expected this summer and a final policy statement anticipated in the fall. The FCA emphasized that until the new regulatory framework is fully implemented, cryptocurrencies largely remain in a regulatory void, governed only by financial promotion and financial crime regulations.

Additionally, the FCA plans to conduct another round of consultations later this year on decentralized finance (DeFi), the operational resilience of distributed ledger technology enterprises, and updates to financial crime guidelines.

data.title: BTTC Bridge Enhances Transaction Tracking Features
data.description: BTTC Bridge has launched an upgraded transaction record page to improve asset tracking for cross-chain users. According to ChainCatcher, the update introduces dual-view modes, 'All Records' and 'In Progress,' along with multi-dimensional filtering tools. These enhancements allow users to search transactions by status, type, and custom date ranges. The page now also features distinct identifiers for deposits, withdrawals, and cross-chain activities, facilitating quicker fund tracking. Users can now access the platform for a clearer cross-chain transaction management experience. The team plans to continue refining product details to support broader on-chain interactions.

data.title: The UK Financial Conduct Authority (FCA) is seeking public opinion on crypto rules ahead of the implementation of new regulations in October 2027.
data.description: The UK Financial Conduct Authority (FCA) is seeking public comment on interpretations of specific digital asset regulatory activities under its crypto roadmap. This consultation covers how activities such as stablecoin issuance, trading platforms, custody, and staking should be brought under regulation. The consultation will close on June 3, 2026, and crypto businesses can apply for FCA authorization from September 30, 2026. The FCA stated that the consultation on future crypto asset regulations is largely complete, and a policy statement will be released this summer. Prior to the implementation of the new legislation in 2027, crypto assets in the UK will remain largely unregulated, except for financial promotion and financial crime purposes.

data.title: RAVE Token Experiences Dramatic Surge Amid Market Manipulation Concerns
data.description: The RAVE token has seen a remarkable increase in value, soaring nearly 80 times over the past week. According to PANews, this surge has raised questions about potential market manipulation and the concentration of token holdings. Concerns have emerged regarding the monopolization of 90% of the tokens and possible on-chain manipulation tactics. The situation highlights the risks associated with the altcoin market, where investors may fall prey to FOMO (fear of missing out) and short-selling traps. As the market continues to evolve, investors are advised to remain vigilant and informed to navigate these challenges effectively.

data.title: Data Science Sees 74% Growth as Candidates Seek Clarity and Certainty
data.description: Data science has experienced a significant growth of 74%. According to PANews, candidates are now prioritizing clear value explanations and certainty over impulsive decisions typically seen during bull markets.

data.title: ECB Meeting Minutes Highlight Improved Inflation Starting Point Compared to 2022
data.description: The European Central Bank (ECB) has released its latest meeting minutes, indicating that the current inflation starting point is more favorable than it was in 2022. According to Jin10, the ECB's assessment suggests a positive shift in the inflationary landscape, which could influence future monetary policy decisions. The minutes reflect the ECB's ongoing efforts to manage inflation and stabilize the economy amid various global challenges. This development comes as central banks worldwide continue to navigate complex economic conditions.

data.title: Renault Prioritizes Growth in India as Key Sales and Export Hub
data.description: Renault is focusing on expanding its presence in India, aiming to establish the country as a significant sales and export hub, according to CEO Francois Provost. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the French carmaker's strategic shift towards the Indian market. This move aligns with Renault's broader strategy to enhance its global footprint and leverage India's growing automotive sector. Provost emphasized the importance of India in Renault's future plans, noting the potential for increased sales and exports from the region. The company is expected to invest in local production and innovation to cater to the Indian market's unique demands. Renault's commitment to India reflects its ambition to tap into emerging markets and drive sustainable growth.

data.description: 1. A whale, after 2.5 months of inactivity, opened a 20x leveraged long position in gold and silver, totaling $11.5 million; 2. IEA warns: European aviation fuel stocks are only enough for 6 weeks, some flights are about to be canceled; 3. Morgan Stanley Bitcoin Trust sees net inflows of over $103 million; 4. data.title: Swedish Central Bank Deputy Governor: Spillover Risk Lower Than 2022 Inflation Levels
data.description: Swedish Central Bank Deputy Governor Jansson has stated that the current risk of spillover effects is lower than the levels observed when inflation began to rise in 2022. According to Jin10, Jansson emphasized that the economic conditions have improved, reducing the likelihood of significant spillover impacts. He noted that the central bank is closely monitoring the situation to ensure stability in the financial markets. Jansson's comments reflect a cautious optimism about the current economic environment compared to the challenges faced in 2022.

data.title: NASA Faces Challenges and Opportunities with Lunar Landers from SpaceX and Blue Origin
data.description: NASA's decision to utilize lunar landers developed by SpaceX and Blue Origin presents both challenges and opportunities for the agency. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the reliance on these unproven technologies as a significant factor in NASA's lunar exploration strategy. The collaboration with these private companies marks a shift in how NASA approaches space missions, potentially accelerating advancements while also introducing risks associated with untested systems. As the agency moves forward, the balance between innovation and reliability will be crucial in achieving successful lunar missions.

data.title: U.S. Oil Companies Face Uncertainty Amid Trump's Policies on Iran and Venezuela
data.description: U.S. oil companies are grappling with uncertainty as U.S. President Donald Trump's policies regarding Iran and Venezuela could lead to a high-risk era for the industry. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the challenges faced by these companies in navigating the geopolitical landscape. The administration's stance on Iran and Venezuela has created a complex environment, leaving many oil firms unsure of their roles and strategies moving forward. As tensions rise, the industry is closely monitoring developments to assess potential impacts on operations and market dynamics.

data.title: Crypto Industry Trends and Predictions for 2026 Q1 Released by TruthX and Animoca Brands
data.description:
On April 16, TruthX, in collaboration with Animoca Brands, released the '2026 Q1 Top 220 KOL Report: Summary and Predictions of Crypto Industry Trends.' According to BlockBeats, the report highlights significant volatility in the crypto market during the first quarter of 2026, driven by geopolitical and macroeconomic uncertainties. Traditional high-valuation narratives are rapidly declining, while new directions such as AI infrastructure and Real World Assets (RWA) are gaining momentum.

The rapid shifts and intense fluctuations in the market, coupled with information overload, make trend assessment challenging. The collective insights of the top 220 global crypto Key Opinion Leaders (KOLs) serve as a crucial reference for understanding industry dynamics. TruthX has conducted structured data analysis on their content, providing forward-looking predictions on subsequent trends, opportunity windows, and risk points.

This report aims to help users quickly rebuild trend frameworks in a volatile market and seize opportunities in the next phase.

data.title: IEA warns: European aviation fuel stocks only last for 6 weeks, some flights may be cancelled.
data.description: According to the Associated Press, International Energy Agency (IEA) Executive Director Fatih Birol said in an interview on Thursday that Europe's aviation fuel supply "may only last about six weeks," and warned that flight cancellations could "soon" occur if oil supplies continue to be disrupted due to the war with Iran. He called the Strait of Hormuz crisis "the biggest energy crisis in history." Birol said, "There used to be a band called 'Terror Strait.' Now it's a real 'Terror Strait,' which will have a significant impact on the global economy. The longer this situation lasts, the greater the negative impact on global economic growth and inflation." Birol stated that this will drive up gasoline, natural gas, and electricity prices, with countries reliant on Middle Eastern energy sources, including Japan, South Korea, India, Pakistan, and Bangladesh, bearing the brunt, before spreading to Europe and the United States. If the strait does not reopen, some European flights may be canceled due to fuel shortages.

data.title: Bitget has launched USD-margined GENIUS perpetual contracts with leverage ranging from 1x to 20x.
data.description: According to an official announcement, Bitget has launched USDT-based GENIUS perpetual contracts with leverage ranging from 1x to 20x. Contract trading bots will also be available simultaneously.
